text:
In a forest deep within an ancient woodland, there lived a unicorn named Elara. She had a shimmering coat of unicorn gold that glowed softly like the setting sun. Every day she would venture into the forest clearing where the ancient oak trees stood silently, as if listening to the secrets of the universe. One particular spring morning, after a shower that left her as glistening as a newly polished sword, Elara felt an inexplicable urge to visit the village.

There was no reason—just an old book she found tucked away among her mother's owl feathers that whispered tales of fairy kings and enchanted forests. Crossing the meadow, she admired the way dandelions painted dappled sunlight on the mossy ground.

She reached the village street late in the afternoon. The sight that greeted her was something akin to a wonderland come to life. Children’s laughter danced through the air like tiny bells; birds sang sweet melodies; and the aroma of fresh bread filled her senses.
